subject: Mathematics
title: Cherry-Picking Data
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Cherry-Picking Data.

hook.text: Show only the data that supports your point and hide the rest. It's dishonest—and very common.

buildup.text: Cherry-picking selects favorable data while ignoring evidence that contradicts the desired conclusion.

discovery.text: A company might highlight one quarter of record profits while hiding three quarters of steady decline.

twist.text: Even honest researchers can cherry-pick unconsciously—seeing what they hope to find in the data.

climax.text: The antidote is pre-registration: declaring what you'll measure before you collect data.

punchline.text: Half the data tells half the truth—which is often a whole lie.

quiz.question: What is cherry-picking in statistics?
options: Selecting only data that supports your conclusion
